hawkish_phrases: ongoing tightening of monetary policy over the forecast period, at a gradual pace and to a limited extent, would be appropriate
The labour market remains tight and annual pay growth...has remained around 3½%
growth in unit wage costs has also risen
excess demand built over the second half of the forecast period
CPI inflation rose slightly to 1.9% in February and is expected to remain close to the 2% target
dovish_phrases: a weaker near-term outlook was expected to lead to a small margin of slack opening up this year
The broad-based softening in global GDP and trade growth has continued
Brexit uncertainties also continue to weigh on confidence and short-term economic activity, notably business investment
Global financial conditions have eased, in part supported by announcements of more accommodative policies in some major economies
other indicators of domestically generated inflation have remained modest
summary: The MPC voted unanimously to hold Bank Rate at 0.75% with a mildly hawkish bias, stating that ongoing gradual tightening would be appropriate if the economy develops in line with projections showing excess demand building over the forecast period.
